target.title: fix(state): avoid sqlite wal on nfs state volumes
target.body:
## Summary

- Detect SQLite database paths on Linux NFS-backed volumes and use rollback journaling instead of WAL.
- Keep existing WAL behavior unchanged for local filesystems, including the default shared state database path.
- Pass the memory-core database path into the shared SQLite journal helper and add regression coverage for the helper plus shared state opener.

Fixes #90491

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** OpenClaw no longer forces SQLite WAL for state databases whose backing volume is reported as Linux NFS, avoiding the WAL-on-NFS path that can corrupt persisted state during Azure Container Apps revision replacement.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with Node `v24.15.0` and pnpm `11.2.2`; real `node:sqlite` `DatabaseSync` opened through `openOpenClawStateDatabase()`, with Linux NFS detection simulated by the same `fs.statfsSync(...).type === 0x6969` contract used by the patch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.readonly-recovery.test.ts`
  - `node --import tsx --input-type=module` smoke that opens a normal shared state DB, then opens another shared state DB after stubbing `fs.statfsSync` to return Linux NFS magic `0x6969`.
  - `corepack pnpm format:check -- src/infra/sqlite-wal.ts src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.ts`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/sqlite-wal.ts src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.ts`
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions`
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`
- **Evidence after fix:** Focused Vitest output:

  ```text
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  14 passed (14)

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  5 passed (5)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 48.07s
  ```

  Memory-core smoke test output:

  ```text
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  9 passed (9)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 22.35s
  ```

  Real `node:sqlite` shared-state opener smoke output:

  ```json
  {
    "normalJournalMode": "wal",
    "normalHasWalSidecar": true,
    "nfsJournalMode": "delete",
    "nfsHasWalSidecar": false,
    "statfsCheckedSuffixes": [
      "<nfs-state>/state"
    ]
  }
  ```

  Autoreview output:

  ```text
  autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
  overall: patch is correct (0.78)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The default local shared state database still uses WAL and creates WAL sidecars. When the same production opener sees the database directory on a simulated Linux NFS volume, it selects `journal_mode=delete` and creates no WAL/SHM sidecar files. The memory-core database path now reaches the same shared helper instead of opening without path context.
- **What was not tested:** Did not run a live Azure Container Apps deployment or a real NFS mount. The NFS branch was proven by stubbing the Linux `statfs` filesystem type returned to the production opener; real ACA/NFS validation remains useful maintainer-side proof.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.readonly-recovery.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx --input-type=module` shared-state opener smoke described above
- `corepack pnpm format:check -- src/infra/sqlite-wal.ts src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/sqlite-wal.ts src/infra/sqlite-wal.test.ts src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-db.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`
